title: Unlimited Sciences | Brand Launch
summary: I joined Unlimited Sciences as an early team member, leading marketing strategy and messaging as the organization grew into one of the leading nonprofits in psychedelic research. The work uses technology, data science, and research at scale to understand psychedelic usage and shape public policy around evidence-based practice.
body: Psychedelics are intimidating and confusing for most people, so the brand had to meet them where they were. We built the platform around approachable messaging, intuitive UX, and data-backed facts so that newcomers and clinicians could find common ground in the same place.

The first study was a collaboration with the Johns Hopkins Center for Psychedelic Research and Consciousness, designed to become the largest real-world study of psilocybin ever conducted. The data feeds back into the brand's public-facing materials, turning research into accessible storytelling.

Destigmatization comes through stories, not statistics, so we launched Psychedelic Diaries as a long-form series following individual journeys. The first chapter is from Deborah, a cancer survivor whose experience reframes what these medicines can do for someone at the end of life.
